description:
h2. Epic Goal
*As a* manufacturer with a long-lived product,
*I want* a tamper-evident, signed 10-year vault of my compliance evidence,
*so that* I can prove an unbroken, independently-witnessed compliance record for the regulatory retention period.

h3. Scope
Vault schema + snapshot capture; hybrid-signed manifests; anti-tamper hash chain; scheduled snapshots; periodic re-verification; customer + admin ledger views; one-off backfill; auto-extend policy.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/retention-ledger.test.ts}} + {{snapshot-schedule.test.ts}} + {{admin-retention-ledger.test.ts}}, *then* they pass.
* *Given* a snapshot, *when* taken, *then* it carries {{.sig}} + {{.sig.mldsa}} and chains via {{previous_hash}}.
* *Given* re-verification, *when* run, *then* hash + signature + RFC3161 token are re-checked and the result stored.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-213…CRAN-221 (schema → signed manifest → hash chain → scheduled → re-verify → customer view → admin view → backfill → auto-extend)

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* the vault schema and a snapshot-capture pipeline,
*so that* point-in-time compliance evidence can be captured and stored.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{pool.ts initDb()}}, *then* {{compliance_snapshots}}, {{retention_ledger}}, {{snapshot_schedules}} exist.
* *Given* a manual trigger, *then* a snapshot is created.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase A foundation.

h3. Sources
P8 Phase A commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* each snapshot ZIP signed with Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65,
*so that* snapshots are cryptographically attributable and quantum-resistant.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a snapshot ZIP, *then* both {{.sig}} and {{.sig.mldsa}} exist beside it.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase B; reuses the hybrid-signing service (CRAN-140).

h3. Sources
P8 Phase B commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* an anti-tamper hash chain over the retention ledger,
*so that* any tampering with the evidence record is detectable.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/retention-ledger.test.ts}}, *then* chain verification is covered.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase C; {{previous_hash}} chains each row; verification job reports broken links.

h3. Sources
P8 Phase C commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* snapshots taken automatically on a schedule,
*so that* evidence accrues without manual effort.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/snapshot-schedule.test.ts}}, *then* it passes.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase D; {{snapshot_schedules}} + nightly scheduler per active product.

h3. Sources
P8 Phase D commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* periodic re-verification of every snapshot,
*so that* I know my historical evidence still verifies years later.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the re-verification job, *then* it runs on schedule and stores the result on {{retention_ledger}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase E; re-hash + re-verify signature + re-verify RFC3161 token.

h3. Sources
P8 Phase E commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* a customer-facing retention-ledger view,
*so that* I can see my own vault state at a glance.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a product with snapshots, *when* viewed, *then* the ledger renders (taken / last-verified / chain status).

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase F; read-only.

h3. Sources
P8 Phase F commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform admin,
*I want* an admin retention-ledger view with integrity warnings,
*so that* I can spot orphan rows and broken chains across all orgs.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/admin-retention-ledger.test.ts}}, *then* it passes.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase G; cross-org with orphan + hash-chain warnings.

h3. Sources
P8 Phase G commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* a one-off backfill of snapshots for pre-vault products,
*so that* products that pre-date the vault still get a complete record.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the funding-run script in {{scripts/}}, *when* run, *then* it produces backfilled rows.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase H; one-off backfill.

h3. Sources
P8 funding-run commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* retention to auto-extend while a product is still active,
*so that* evidence for a live product is never lost when a scheduled end approaches.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a fixture product approaching its scheduled end, *when* still active, *then* the end-date auto-extends.

h3. Implementation Notes
Phase I; prevents premature expiry.

h3. Sources
P8 auto-extend commits.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* prospective customer on the public site,
*I want* free self-assessment wizards for CRA, NIS2, Importer and PQC readiness,
*so that* I can gauge my regulatory readiness publicly and become a lead, with frictionless verified-email reuse.

h3. Scope
Public CRA / NIS2 / Importer Art. 18 / PQC self-assessment wizards on the welcome site; cross-system verified-emails (90-day TTL) across 6 flows; assessment landing page + launch-list subscription; 12 measured growth funnels.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/conformity-assessment.test.ts}}, *then* the CRA assessment is covered; the {{/conformity/*}} routes render each wizard.
* *Given* an already-verified email, *when* it returns, *then* the 6 welcome-site flows skip code entry.
* *Given* a launch-list signup, *when* submitted, *then* it writes to {{welcome_leads}}.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-222…CRAN-227 (CRA / NIS2 / Importer / PQC / verified-emails / landing + launch-list)

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* prospective customer,
*I want* a public CRA self-assessment wizard,
*so that* I get a CRA-readiness score + recommendations before signing up.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/conformity-assessment.test.ts}}, *then* the CRA assessment is covered.
* *Given* {{/conformity/cra}}, *then* the wizard renders.

h3. Implementation Notes
Walks product details + supply chain + obligations → score + recommendations.

h3. Sources
P9 CRA-assessment commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* in-scope NIS2 entity,
*I want* a public NIS2 self-assessment wizard,
*so that* I can gauge my NIS2 readiness on the same pattern.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{/conformity/nis2}}, *then* the wizard renders.

h3. Implementation Notes
Same wizard pattern as CRA.

h3. Sources
P9 NIS2-assessment commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* importer,
*I want* an Importer Art. 18 self-assessment,
*so that* I understand my importer obligations and whether my manufacturer is compliant.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{/conformity/importer}}, *then* the wizard renders.

h3. Implementation Notes
Verifies manufacturer compliance + distributor/importer obligations (CRA Art. 18).

h3. Sources
P9 importer-assessment commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* security-conscious prospect,
*I want* a PQC-readiness self-assessment,
*so that* I get a post-quantum readiness score against NIST PQC standards.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{/conformity/pqc}}, *then* the wizard renders.
* *Given* the page, *then* it carries the 'practises what it preaches' trust banner.

h3. Implementation Notes
Crypto-inventory inputs → classify against NIST PQC → score.

h3. Sources
P9 PQC-assessment commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* returning visitor,
*I want* verified emails recognised across the welcome site and main app,
*so that* I don't re-verify my email on every funnel I touch.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the 6 welcome-site flows, *when* an already-verified email returns, *then* code entry is skipped.
* *Given* {{verified_emails}}, *then* it populates cross-flow (90-day TTL).

h3. Implementation Notes
{{welcome/lib/verified-emails.js}} checks welcome DB + main-app {{users.email_verified}}; {{/send-code}} returns {{alreadyVerified}}; {{/verify}} accepts {{skipCode}} with server re-validation; no enumeration; disposables excluded.

h3. Sources
Session 59 verified-emails commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform owner,
*I want* an assessment landing page with launch-list subscription,
*so that* I capture leads with shareable previews and measure the growth funnels.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the landing page, *then* it renders with OG/Twitter share tags.
* *Given* a launch-list signup, *then* it writes to {{welcome_leads}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
12 growth funnels measured end-to-end.

h3. Sources
P9 landing-page + launch-list commits.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* manufacturer of a regulated product,
*I want* the whole Art. 14 chain automated from threat-intel detection to authorised submission,
*so that* an actively-exploited vulnerability auto-creates an attested, deadline-tracked Art. 14 report without me watching the feeds.

h3. Scope
KEV + EPSS ingestion; scan-time enrichment; KEV/EPSS-driven prioritisation; UI surfacing; {{actively_exploited}} auto-flag; automated Art. 14 trigger; RFC3161 awareness attestation; per-product regulatory-state view; submit-with-authorisation + RFC3161 on stage submit.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/threat-intel.test.ts}} + {{cra-trigger-engine.test.ts}} + {{regulatory-state.test.ts}} + {{submission-attestation.test.ts}}, *then* they pass.
* *Given* an actively-exploited finding on a regulated product, *when* detected, *then* a {{cra_reports}} row is auto-created with {{awareness_at}} + RFC3161 token and the 24h clock starts.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-228…CRAN-236 (KEV/EPSS ingestion → enrichment → prioritisation → UI → auto-flag → trigger → awareness attestation → regulatory state → submit-with-auth)

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* scheduled ingestion of CISA KEV and FIRST EPSS feeds,
*so that* the platform knows which CVEs are actively exploited and their exploitation probability.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/threat-intel.test.ts}}, *then* ingestion + refresh are covered.
* *Given* a scheduled refresh, *then* {{kev_catalogue}} is populated.

h3. Implementation Notes
P10a-1; {{kev_catalogue}} + {{epss_scores}}.

h3. Sources
P10a-1 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* findings enriched with KEV/EPSS state at scan time,
*so that* each finding carries its exploitation signal, not just CVSS.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a finding row, *then* it exposes {{kev: bool}}, {{epss_score}}, {{epss_percentile}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
P10a-2; joined to KEV+EPSS at scan time.

h3. Sources
P10a-2 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* findings prioritised by KEV/EPSS over raw CVSS,
*so that* I act on what's actually being exploited first.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the risk-findings sort, *then* it is KEV-first, EPSS-percentile-desc, CVSS-desc.

h3. Implementation Notes
P10a-3; an EPSS-high finding outranks a high-CVSS no-signal finding.

h3. Sources
P10a-3 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* KEV / actively-exploited / EPSS surfaced in the findings UI,
*so that* I can see exploitation status at a glance.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the risk-findings table, *then* it renders a KEV badge + EPSS percentile column.

h3. Implementation Notes
P10a-4; KEV badge + actively-exploited chip + EPSS percentile.

h3. Sources
P10a-4 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* an actively_exploited flag auto-derived on CRA reports,
*so that* reports created from exploited findings are flagged automatically.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a report created from a finding, *then* {{cra_reports.actively_exploited}} is populated from KEV/EPSS state.

h3. Implementation Notes
P10a-5.

h3. Sources
P10a-5 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* Art. 14 auto-triggered when an actively-exploited vuln hits a regulated product,
*so that* the statutory clock starts and stakeholders are alerted the moment exploitation is detected.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/cra-trigger-engine.test.ts}}, *then* it passes.
* *Given* detection, *then* a {{cra_reports}} row is auto-created with {{awareness_at = NOW()}}, stakeholders notified, 24h track started (integration-verified).

h3. Implementation Notes
P10b-1.

h3. Sources
Commit {{949aef2}} (2026-05-06 09:34).

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* an RFC3161 timestamp on the awareness moment of auto-triggered reports,
*so that* the 24h clock has an independent witness of when awareness began.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* an auto-trigger, *then* {{cra_reports.awareness_rfc3161_token}} is populated.

h3. Implementation Notes
P10b-2.

h3. Sources
Commit {{b8441c0}} (2026-05-06 09:42).

h3. User Story
*As a* manufacturer,
*I want* the dedicated per-product regulatory-state surface,
*so that* I see the active Art. 14 track and countdown for a product.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/regulatory-state.test.ts}}, *then* it passes.

h3. Implementation Notes
P10c; 24h/72h/14d countdown chips. (Report-flow side is CRAN-133.)

h3. Sources
Commit {{fe1ba49}} (2026-05-06 10:13).

h3. User Story
*As a* compliance officer,
*I want* submit-with-authorisation + RFC3161 on each Art. 14 stage submit,
*so that* every submission records who authorised it, when, with an independent timestamp.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{services/submission-attestation.test.ts}}, *then* it passes.

h3. Implementation Notes
P10d. (Report-flow side is CRAN-132.)

h3. Sources
Commit {{290d85f}} (2026-05-06 10:29).
